visa_types.name: National D Study
visa_types.documents_required:
This category is solely subject to applicants who have received the information by the Austrian Embassy that their residence permit has been approved. This notification will be checked upon submission of the application for the national visa “D” – Abh.AT. All other applicants must book their appointments under the category of short term visa (type C) here (link VFS) (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/ken/en/aut) or long term visa (type D) here (link embassy) (https://www.bmeia.gv.at/en/austrian-embassy-nairobi/travelling-to-austria/entrance-and-residence/how-to-apply).
Kindly visit the link to view the document checklist of requirements before you scheduled appointment here
Kindly visit the link to view the list of approved Insurance provided (PDF Link)
visa_types.processing_time:
Usually, processing time for visa applications is 10 working days (starting from the time when the Embassy or Consulate received the application, not including the transportation time), counting from when all requested documents have been submitted. This period may be extended in specific cases, notably when further scrutiny of the application is required, for example, when an applicant must come to an interview or submit additional documentation requested by the Embassy or Consulate. During certain times of the year, the processing time can be longer due to the high volume of applications. It is recommended to submit your visa application at least two weeks prior to date of travel date and if possible, even earlier ahead of the holiday period. 
Please note that the application processing time and decisions on visa applications are the sole prerogative of the Embassy or Consulate. VFS Global has no role to play in these areas of the application process.
visa_types.overview:
Overview
Purpose: Long-term education, academic research, or vocational training exceeding 90 days.
If you intend to stay in Austria for more than 90 days and up to 6 months AND you are not a citizen of a European Union or European Free Trade Association member state, you need to apply for a national Austrian visa (Visa D).
Long-Stay Visa Extension: A long-stay (type D) visa cannot be extended in Austria. For stays exceeding 6 months, apply for a residence permit directly at the local Embassy/Consulate.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
Purpose: Long-term education, academic research, or vocational training exceeding 90 days.
If you intend to stay in Austria for more than 90 days and up to 6 months AND you are not a citizen of a European Union or European Free Trade Association member state, you need to apply for a national Austrian visa (Visa D).
Long-Stay Visa Extension: A long-stay (type D) visa cannot be extended in Austria. For stays exceeding 6 months, apply for a residence permit directly at the local Embassy/Consulate.

## Visa Fees

[National D Work]

## Documents Required
This category is solely subject to applicants who have received the information by the Austrian Embassy that their residence permit has been approved. This notification will be checked upon submission of the application for the national visa “D” – Abh.AT. All other applicants must book their appointments under the category of short term visa (type C) here (link VFS) (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/ken/en/aut) or long term visa (type D) here (link embassy) (https://www.bmeia.gv.at/en/austrian-embassy-nairobi/travelling-to-austria/entrance-and-residence/how-to-apply).
Kindly visit the link to view the document checklist of requirements before you scheduled appointment here
Kindly visit the link to view the list of approved Insurance provided (PDF Link)

## Photo Specifications
General 
- The photograph       for each visa applicant must be an un-mounted, full face photo, as       described below
- The photograph       must have been taken within the last six months.
- One photograph       must be submitted. The photograph should be glued to the application form
Photo Size 
          The photo for each visa applicant submitted must measure: 
- 35 mm x 45 mm       with the head centered in the frame.
- The head       (measured from the top of the hair to the bottom of the chin) should       measure about 3 mm
Photo Appearance 
- The photograph       must be a full-face front view in which the visa applicant is facing the       camera directly.
- The face should       cover about 70-80 percent of the photo area.
- The photograph       MUST be in color and have only a white background. Photos with dark, busy,       or patterned backgrounds will not be accepted.
- In general, the       applicant's head, including both face and hair, should be shown from the       crown of the head to the tip of the chin on top and bottom and from       hairline side-to-side. It is preferable that ears be exposed
- Sunglasses or       other wear, which detracts from the face, are not acceptable unless       required for medical reasons (an eye patch, for example).
- A photograph       depicting a person wearing a traditional facemask or veil that does not       permit adequate identification is not acceptable.
- Kindly download the Photo Specification file for more information
Note:
Please follow these instructions carefully. If  photographs presented do not meet these requirements your application will be  considered incomplete

## Processing Time
Usually, processing time for visa applications is 10 working days (starting from the time when the Embassy or Consulate received the application, not including the transportation time), counting from when all requested documents have been submitted. This period may be extended in specific cases, notably when further scrutiny of the application is required, for example, when an applicant must come to an interview or submit additional documentation requested by the Embassy or Consulate. During certain times of the year, the processing time can be longer due to the high volume of applications. It is recommended to submit your visa application at least two weeks prior to date of travel date and if possible, even earlier ahead of the holiday period. 
Please note that the application processing time and decisions on visa applications are the sole prerogative of the Embassy or Consulate. VFS Global has no role to play in these areas of the application process.

## Download Form
 Please visit the below link to download the application form
https://www.bmeia.gv.at/en/austrian-embassy-nairobi/travelling-to-austria/entrance-and-residence/how-to-apply (https://www.bmeia.gv.at/en/austrian-embassy-nairobi/travelling-to-austria/entrance-and-residence/how-to-apply)

visa_types.overview:
Overview
Purpose: Extended medical care or specialized treatment requiring a stay beyond 90 days.
If you intend to stay in Austria for more than 90 days and up to 6 months AND you are not a citizen of a European Union or European Free Trade Association member state, you need to apply for a national Austrian visa (Visa D).
Long-Stay Visa Extension: A long-stay (type D) visa cannot be extended in Austria. For stays exceeding 6 months, apply for a residence permit directly at the local Embassy/Consulate.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
Purpose: Extended medical care or specialized treatment requiring a stay beyond 90 days.
If you intend to stay in Austria for more than 90 days and up to 6 months AND you are not a citizen of a European Union or European Free Trade Association member state, you need to apply for a national Austrian visa (Visa D).
Long-Stay Visa Extension: A long-stay (type D) visa cannot be extended in Austria. For stays exceeding 6 months, apply for a residence permit directly at the local Embassy/Consulate.

## Visa Fees

[National D Work]

## Documents Required
This category is solely subject to applicants who have received the information by the Austrian Embassy that their residence permit has been approved. This notification will be checked upon submission of the application for the national visa “D” – Abh.AT. All other applicants must book their appointments under the category of short term visa (type C) here (link VFS) (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/ken/en/aut) or long term visa (type D) here (link embassy) (https://www.bmeia.gv.at/en/austrian-embassy-nairobi/travelling-to-austria/entrance-and-residence/how-to-apply).
Kindly visit the link to view the document checklist of requirements before you scheduled appointment here
Kindly visit the link to view the list of approved Insurance provided (PDF Link)

## Photo Specifications
General 
- The photograph       for each visa applicant must be an un-mounted, full face photo, as       described below
- The photograph       must have been taken within the last six months.

Photo Size 
          The photo for each visa applicant submitted must measure: 
- 35 mm x 45 mm       with the head centered in the frame.
- The head       (measured from the top of the hair to the bottom of the chin) should       measure about 3 mm

- The face should       cover about 70-80 percent of the photo area.
- The photograph       MUST be in color and have only a white background. Photos with dark, busy,       or patterned backgrounds will not be accepted.
- In general, the       applicant's head, including both face and hair, should be shown from the       crown of the head to the tip of the chin on top and bottom and from       hairline side-to-side. It is preferable that ears be exposed
- Sunglasses or       other wear, which detracts from the face, are not acceptable unless       required for medical reasons (an eye patch, for example).
- A photograph       depicting a person wearing a traditional facemask or veil that does not       permit adequate identification is not acceptable.
- Kindly download the Photo Specification file for more information

Please follow these instructions carefully. If  photographs presented do not meet these requirements your application will be  considered incomplete

## Processing Time
Usually, processing time for visa applications is 10 working days (starting from the time when the Embassy or Consulate received the application, not including the transportation time), counting from when all requested documents have been submitted. This period may be extended in specific cases, notably when further scrutiny of the application is required, for example, when an applicant must come to an interview or submit additional documentation requested by the Embassy or Consulate. During certain times of the year, the processing time can be longer due to the high volume of applications. It is recommended to submit your visa application at least two weeks prior to date of travel date and if possible, even earlier ahead of the holiday period. 
Please note that the application processing time and decisions on visa applications are the sole prerogative of the Embassy or Consulate. VFS Global has no role to play in these areas of the application process.

## Download Form
 Please visit the below link to download the application form
https://www.bmeia.gv.at/en/austrian-embassy-nairobi/travelling-to-austria/entrance-and-residence/how-to-apply (https://www.bmeia.gv.at/en/austrian-embassy-nairobi/travelling-to-austria/entrance-and-residence/how-to-apply)

visa_types.name: Schengen visa type C
visa_types.documents_required:
Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/ken/en/aut/travel-insurance)
Applicants must submit the following documents as a minimum  requirement when applying for a Schengen visa:
Fully completed Application Form and  signed personally by the applicant 
One recent passport  photograph
Passport (valid for at least three  months after the expiration of the visa applied for) and photocopies of  passport bio-data and any previous Schengen, US and UK visas
Residence permits must be valid AT  LEAST THREE MONTHS after the expiry date of visa/return travel date
Travel health insurance valid for all Schengen states, covering all risks, stating names, days covered amount cover (minimum of EUR 30.000)
Non-refundable visa-fee paid via Mpesa at the Visa application centre.
Original or certified bank  statements from the applicant for last three months
Flight reservation
Letter from your employer or  Business Registration Certificate ( stating salary and date of employment )
Additional requirements for Minors
Letter from the school/university  concerning leave of absence
Copy of full birth certificate  (indicating both parent’s names) accompanied by parents passport/ID copies( for  all children under 18 irrespective if they are travelling with both parents or  not)
A letter of consent and parents passport/ID copies are required if child does not accompany both parents, the parent(s) who   is (are) not traveling, must complete the affidavit of consent form/letter
Requirements as a tourist
Statement of the employer ( stating  salary and date of employment ) and proof of sufficient financial means
Proof of financial means: bank  statement for the last three months
Hotel reservation (should clearly  state name, arrival and departure date, telephone number and address of the  hotel)
Upon invitation of a person residing in Austria
 Sponsorship form  ("Elektronische Verpflichtungserklärung") from the person to be  visited
Statement of the employer and/or  proof of sufficient financial means of the invited person
Non-Austrian Citizen must provide  additional proof of valid Austria residence permit (copy) is required. 
Upon invitation by a company/business partner residing in  Austria
Proof of sufficient financial means  (bank statement) 
Sponsorship Form  ("elektronische Verpflichtungserklärung") from the company to be  visited or abstract of commercial register ("Handelsregister") (max.  3 months old)
Processing fees 
To attend a seminar or sports event
Sponsorship Form  ("elektronische Verpflichtungserklärung") from the inviting  person/company
Official invitation from organizer  in Austria
Letter of recommendation of Kenyan  counterpart/organization
Please note: Incorrect or incomplete information in applications lead to the rejection of the application. There is no legal right to a visa. The applicant must submit through documentation credible evidence of the purpose of the visit and the willingness to return to the country of residence. 
List of approved insurance companies
Checklist
visa_types.visa_fees:
Visa Fees applicable for Schengen visas types are listed in the table below:

[Table:Schengen visa type C]
The following group of applicants shall apply straight at the Embassy/consular section:
- applicants who require an Austrian residence permit
- applicants who require a visa in accordance with § 35 (3) of the 2005 Asylum Act
- The applicable VFS service fee in Kenyan Shillings is as per the current exchange rate. It is subject to change without notice. The fee is non-refundable.
- VFS service fee must be paid online at the time of booking an appointment. Visa fee and optional service fees will be paid via M-pesa at Visa Application Centre after completion of documents checks by the VFS Counter Staff.
- Visa Application Centre offers an optional service at a nominal additional cost. Please see more detail from 
Additional Services (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/ken/en/aut/additional-services)
 tab.
- Please note that visa fees are subject to change monthly.
visa_types.processing_time:
Usually, processing time for visa applications is 10 working days (starting from the time when the Embassy or Consulate received the application, not including the transportation time), counting from when all requested documents have been submitted. This period may be extended in specific cases, notably when further scrutiny of the application is required, for example, when an applicant must come to an interview or submit additional documentation requested by the Embassy or Consulate. During certain times of the year, the processing time can be longer due to the high volume of applications. It is recommended to submit your visa application at least two weeks prior to date of travel date and if possible, even earlier ahead of the holiday period. 
Please note that the application processing time and decisions on visa applications are the sole prerogative of the Embassy or Consulate. VFS Global has no role to play in these areas of the application process.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview

## Tourist Visa
This visa allows people to visit Austria for a holiday or recreation,. This visa may also be used for other short-term non-work purposes.
This visa allows people to visit Austria to visit family and/or friends for a stay up to 90 days 
It is mandatory to apply at least 15 Days before your intended travel date.  

## Business Visa
For applicants seeking to engage in business visitor activities including:-
- Making general business or government enquiries
- Contract Negotiations
- Government visits
- Attending conference, Job interviews or Professional examinations
Invitation of a Person residing in Austria 
This visa allows people to visit Austria upon invitation from an Austrian Resident.
Invitation by a Company/Business Partner residing in Austria
For applicants seeking to engage in business visitor activities including:-
- Making general business or government enquiries
- Contract Negotiations
- Government visits
- Attending conference, Job interviews or Professional examinations
It is mandatory to apply at least 15 Days before your intended travel date.  

## Seminar or Sports Event
This visa allows people to visit Austria for participation of a sports activity.

## Visa Fees
Visa Fees applicable for Schengen visas types are listed in the table below:

[Table:Schengen visa type C]
The following group of applicants shall apply straight at the Embassy/consular section:
- applicants who require an Austrian residence permit
- applicants who require a visa in accordance with § 35 (3) of the 2005 Asylum Act
- The applicable VFS service fee in Kenyan Shillings is as per the current exchange rate. It is subject to change without notice. The fee is non-refundable.
- VFS service fee must be paid online at the time of booking an appointment. Visa fee and optional service fees will be paid via M-pesa at Visa Application Centre after completion of documents checks by the VFS Counter Staff.
- Visa Application Centre offers an optional service at a nominal additional cost. Please see more detail from 
Additional Services (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/ken/en/aut/additional-services)
 tab.
- Please note that visa fees are subject to change monthly.

## Documents Required

## Required documents
Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/ken/en/aut/travel-insurance)
Applicants must submit the following documents as a minimum  requirement when applying for a Schengen visa:
Fully completed Application Form and  signed personally by the applicant 
One recent passport  photograph
Passport (valid for at least three  months after the expiration of the visa applied for) and photocopies of  passport bio-data and any previous Schengen, US and UK visas
Residence permits must be valid AT  LEAST THREE MONTHS after the expiry date of visa/return travel date
Travel health insurance valid for all Schengen states, covering all risks, stating names, days covered amount cover (minimum of EUR 30.000)
Non-refundable visa-fee paid via Mpesa at the Visa application centre.
Original or certified bank  statements from the applicant for last three months
Flight reservation
Letter from your employer or  Business Registration Certificate ( stating salary and date of employment )
Additional requirements for Minors
Letter from the school/university  concerning leave of absence
Copy of full birth certificate  (indicating both parent’s names) accompanied by parents passport/ID copies( for  all children under 18 irrespective if they are travelling with both parents or  not)
A letter of consent and parents passport/ID copies are required if child does not accompany both parents, the parent(s) who   is (are) not traveling, must complete the affidavit of consent form/letter
Requirements as a tourist
Statement of the employer ( stating  salary and date of employment ) and proof of sufficient financial means
Proof of financial means: bank  statement for the last three months
Hotel reservation (should clearly  state name, arrival and departure date, telephone number and address of the  hotel)
Upon invitation of a person residing in Austria
 Sponsorship form  ("Elektronische Verpflichtungserklärung") from the person to be  visited
Statement of the employer and/or  proof of sufficient financial means of the invited person
Non-Austrian Citizen must provide  additional proof of valid Austria residence permit (copy) is required. 
Upon invitation by a company/business partner residing in  Austria
Proof of sufficient financial means  (bank statement) 
Sponsorship Form  ("elektronische Verpflichtungserklärung") from the company to be  visited or abstract of commercial register ("Handelsregister") (max.  3 months old)
Processing fees 
To attend a seminar or sports event
Sponsorship Form  ("elektronische Verpflichtungserklärung") from the inviting  person/company
Official invitation from organizer  in Austria
Letter of recommendation of Kenyan  counterpart/organization
Please note: Incorrect or incomplete information in applications lead to the rejection of the application. There is no legal right to a visa. The applicant must submit through documentation credible evidence of the purpose of the visit and the willingness to return to the country of residence. 
List of approved insurance companies
Checklist

## Photo Specifications
General 
- The photograph       for each visa applicant must be an un-mounted, full face photo, as       described below
- The photograph       must have been taken within the last six months.
- One photograph       must be submitted. The photograph should be glued to the application form
Photo Size 
          The photo for each visa applicant submitted must measure: 
- 35 mm x 45 mm       with the head centered in the frame.
- The head       (measured from the top of the hair to the bottom of the chin) should       measure about 3 mm
Photo Appearance 
- The photograph       must be a full-face front view in which the visa applicant is facing the       camera directly.
- The face should       cover about 70-80 percent of the photo area.
- The photograph       MUST be in color and have only a white background. Photos with dark, busy,       or patterned backgrounds will not be accepted.
- In general, the       applicant's head, including both face and hair, should be shown from the       crown of the head to the tip of the chin on top and bottom and from       hairline side-to-side. It is preferable that ears be exposed
- Sunglasses or       other wear, which detracts from the face, are not acceptable unless       required for medical reasons (an eye patch, for example).
- A photograph       depicting a person wearing a traditional facemask or veil that does not       permit adequate identification is not acceptable.
- Kindly download the Photo Specification file for more information
Note:
Please follow these instructions carefully. If  photographs presented do not meet these requirements your application will be  considered incomplete

## Processing Time
Usually, processing time for visa applications is 10 working days (starting from the time when the Embassy or Consulate received the application, not including the transportation time), counting from when all requested documents have been submitted. This period may be extended in specific cases, notably when further scrutiny of the application is required, for example, when an applicant must come to an interview or submit additional documentation requested by the Embassy or Consulate. During certain times of the year, the processing time can be longer due to the high volume of applications. It is recommended to submit your visa application at least two weeks prior to date of travel date and if possible, even earlier ahead of the holiday period. 
Please note that the application processing time and decisions on visa applications are the sole prerogative of the Embassy or Consulate. VFS Global has no role to play in these areas of the application process.

## Download Form
       When you apply for a visa the "Application for Schengen Visa" and the "Application for Schengen Visa,

Supplementary Information" forms must be submitted together. Links to both forms can be found below:

- Application for Schengen Visa
- Checklist
- Affidavit of parental consent
- Cooperative Bank Consent verification form

This period may be extended in specific cases, notably when further scrutiny of the application is required, for example, when an applicant must come to an interview or submit additional documentation requested by the Embassy or Consulate. During certain times of the year, the processing time can be longer due to the high volume of applications. It is recommended to submit your visa application at least two weeks prior to date of travel date and if possible, even earlier ahead of the holiday period. 
Please note that the application processing time and decisions on visa applications are the sole prerogative of the Embassy or Consulate. VFS Global has no role to play in these areas of the application process.
visa_types.overview:
Overview
Researchers intending to apply under this category should reach out to the Embassy directly, which will inform the applicant about the further steps: nairobi-ka@bmeia.gv.at (mailto: nairobi-ka@bmeia.gv.at)
Upon receiving the approval for submission, the application has to be submitted through VFS.
Please note: Applications need to be lodged in-person and cannot be submitted via a third person.
If you intend to stay in Austria for more than 90 days and up to 6 months AND you are not a citizen of a European Union or European Free Trade Association member state, you need to apply for a national Austrian visa (Visa D).
Long-Stay Visa Extension: A long-stay (type D) visa cannot be extended in Austria. For stays exceeding 6 months, apply for a residence permit directly at the local Embassy/Consulate.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
Researchers intending to apply under this category should reach out to the Embassy directly, which will inform the applicant about the further steps: nairobi-ka@bmeia.gv.at (mailto: nairobi-ka@bmeia.gv.at)
Upon receiving the approval for submission, the application has to be submitted through VFS.
Please note: Applications need to be lodged in-person and cannot be submitted via a third person.
If you intend to stay in Austria for more than 90 days and up to 6 months AND you are not a citizen of a European Union or European Free Trade Association member state, you need to apply for a national Austrian visa (Visa D).
Long-Stay Visa Extension: A long-stay (type D) visa cannot be extended in Austria. For stays exceeding 6 months, apply for a residence permit directly at the local Embassy/Consulate.

## Visa Fees

[National D Work]

## Documents Required
This category is solely subject to applicants who have received the information by the Austrian Embassy that their residence permit has been approved. This notification will be checked upon submission of the application for the national visa “D” – Abh.AT. All other applicants must book their appointments under the category of short term visa (type C) here (link VFS) (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/ken/en/aut) or long term visa (type D) here (link embassy) (https://www.bmeia.gv.at/en/austrian-embassy-nairobi/travelling-to-austria/entrance-and-residence/how-to-apply).
Kindly visit the link to view the document checklist of requirements before you scheduled appointment here
Kindly visit the link to view the list of approved Insurance provided (PDF Link)

## Photo Specifications
General 
- The photograph       for each visa applicant must be an un-mounted, full face photo, as       described below
- The photograph       must have been taken within the last six months.
- One photograph       must be submitted. The photograph should be glued to the application form
Photo Size 
          The photo for each visa applicant submitted must measure: 
- 35 mm x 45 mm       with the head centered in the frame.
- The head       (measured from the top of the hair to the bottom of the chin) should       measure about 3 mm
Photo Appearance 
- The photograph       must be a full-face front view in which the visa applicant is facing the       camera directly.
- The face should       cover about 70-80 percent of the photo area.
- The photograph       MUST be in color and have only a white background. Photos with dark, busy,       or patterned backgrounds will not be accepted.
- In general, the       applicant's head, including both face and hair, should be shown from the       crown of the head to the tip of the chin on top and bottom and from       hairline side-to-side. It is preferable that ears be exposed
- Sunglasses or       other wear, which detracts from the face, are not acceptable unless       required for medical reasons (an eye patch, for example).
- A photograph       depicting a person wearing a traditional facemask or veil that does not       permit adequate identification is not acceptable.
- Kindly download the Photo Specification file for more information
Note:
Please follow these instructions carefully. If  photographs presented do not meet these requirements your application will be  considered incomplete

## Processing Time
Usually, processing time for visa applications is 10 working days (starting from the time when the Embassy or Consulate received the application, not including the transportation time), counting from when all requested documents have been submitted. This period may be extended in specific cases, notably when further scrutiny of the application is required, for example, when an applicant must come to an interview or submit additional documentation requested by the Embassy or Consulate. During certain times of the year, the processing time can be longer due to the high volume of applications. It is recommended to submit your visa application at least two weeks prior to date of travel date and if possible, even earlier ahead of the holiday period. 
Please note that the application processing time and decisions on visa applications are the sole prerogative of the Embassy or Consulate. VFS Global has no role to play in these areas of the application process.

## Download Form
 Please visit the below link to download the application form
https://www.bmeia.gv.at/en/austrian-embassy-nairobi/travelling-to-austria/entrance-and-residence/how-to-apply (https://www.bmeia.gv.at/en/austrian-embassy-nairobi/travelling-to-austria/entrance-and-residence/how-to-apply)

visa_types.name: Visa Types
visa_types.overview:
- Short stay visa
- 
(1-90 days, single, double or multiple entry).
- Long stay visa
- 
Visas for visits exceeding 90 days are National visas, and are issued by member states in accordance with national legislation.
- Airport transit visas
- 
Required by the nationals of certain countries.
- 
Job Seeker National D Visa
- 
Kindly be advised that if you intend to apply for the Austrian “Job Seeker” National D Visa, you are required to contact the Embassy for guidance on your request using the following email address: nairobi-ka@bmeia.gv.at (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/one-pager/austria/kenya/english/nairobi-ka@bmeia.gv.at)
It is mandatory to apply at least 2 weeks before your intended travel date.
Applicants are requested to read the requisite documents carefully that needs to be submitted together with your visa application. Please make sure that you have all the documents indicated in the list, since decision could be delayed and deferred if one or several documents are missing.
